floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: t/u round-trip is byte-real (target has same-slot lw/sw pairs) -> respell as setVector idiom; nested do-while(0) wrappers likely load-bearing sched fences (sanctionable w/ FAKE per

Root-caused the whole function: the do-while(0) wrappers are sched.c:2066 TOTAL scheduling barriers (flush_pending_lists + all-reg deps), NOT the sanctioned reorg.c do-while(0) mechanism -- so the rul

Both named frontier levers KILLED with measurements (statement order: 14 orders, 58-67, cannot pin the copies; pending-list flush: real but structurally too late) — and s2's kill of aggregate typing i

frontier.hypothesis: The copied regions are members of a WIDER enclosing object, so every store sits at a NON-ZERO offset from that object's symbol — and the offset-0 collision that defeats combine (and costs the three at-form luis) never arises. This is now the load-bearing question, and it supersedes s2's belief that the region cannot be an aggregate.
frontier.mechanism: The offset-0 CSE collision happens ONLY when a stored word sits at offset 0 OF THE VERY OBJECT BEING BLOCK-COPIED (that is the one case where the store's address pseudo is the bare symbol_ref the copy also needs). If the copied region is at, say, bigobj+0x30, the store's address is (const (plus (symbol_ref bigobj) 0x30)) — a DIFFERENT rtx from the copy's base only if the copy's base is a different offset... so the enclosing object must be chosen such that no stored word coincides with a copy base. Critically, at-form addressing SURVIVES bigobj+N (s2's H8: %lo(D_80101FB0) and %lo(bigobj+0x30) are the same reloc after linking), so the target's at-form stores do NOT rule this out.
frontier.next_probe: Read the .data/.bss layout around 0x80101F80-0x80102120 (splat's segment + bb2.ld + symbol_addrs.txt) and every OTHER function that touches this region, to settle the real object boundaries. Then declare the honest enclosing aggregate and re-measure w1 with the stores at non-zero offsets. Compare against asm/6CAC.s:5019-5147 with tmp/grind/func_8001C624/s3/vdiff.py (which already patches header + src decls and restores them).

frontier.hypothesis: There is an honest aggregate spelling in which the offset-0 store's address pseudo stays SINGLE-USE, so combine folds it back to at-form and the three luis return (125 -> 128, then num_regs 3->2 brings it to exactly 127).
frontier.mechanism: The pseudo is multi-use only because the block copy needs the SAME bare symbol in a register (copy_addr_to_reg). Any spelling in which the copied object's base is not itself a stored lvalue in this function breaks the collision — e.g. a wider struct where the copy source is `obj.vec` at a non-zero offset while the zeroed word is a different member.
frontier.next_probe: Build the enclosing-struct variant from frontier #1 and check the RTL: confirm the offset-0 store's address pseudo has ONE use in the -ds (post-CSE) dump, and that the emitted store is `lui at; sw zero,%lo(...)($at)`. tmp/grind/func_8001C624/s3/rtl.py already dumps -dr/-ds/-dS for any variant.

frontier.hypothesis: Block-copy chunking (num_regs=3 vs the target's 2) is downstream of live-range pressure and will resolve itself once the addressing deltas are fixed.
frontier.mechanism: mips.md's movstrsi_internal always requests 4 scratch regs; mips.c's output_block_move (line 2445) counts how many do NOT collide with the two address registers (safe_regs) and recurses with that count. The target got 2 (lw,lw,sw,sw pairs); the aggregate form gets 3. This is a register-allocator outcome, not a C-level choice.
frontier.next_probe: Do NOT attack in isolation — it is not spellable in C. Re-check it only AFTER frontier #1/#2 land; if it persists once the schedule and live ranges match the target, THEN dump the post-reload RTL and investigate which pseudo collides.

judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): Three distinct cheat constructs found — two unannotated do-while(0) wrappers (one with variant spelling `0 != 0`) and a dead load/store round-trip pair — all permuter-discovered, all framed in the commit by GCC-scheduling intent rather than program spec. Worker must redo the function in pure C, dropping the do-while wrappers and the t/u round-trips; the struct-copy Blk16/Blk12 pattern and the outer sequencing are clean and can stay.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)

Do NOT commit candidate.c's wrapper form. Next session: (1) write the s2 measurements into the DURABLE ledger — hypotheses.md and evidence.md entries and a state.json s2 floor_history entry, with the sandbox numbers, so exhaustion is auditable by someone other than the author; (2) work the three named frontier avenues (sched.c pending-list flush, whole-region struct typing in the header, directed permuter on the anchored base) to a documented dead end. The setVector respell and the `0 != 0` -> `0` normalisation are approved and may be carried forward. If and only if all three avenues are spent and recorded, resubmit a ruling request with the complete ledger — the nested wrappers can then be re-considered under do-while-zero-exception on their merits.
